entry.title: Washington Post: 16 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russian spy tried infiltrating war crimes court, Dutch authorities say
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Macron and Scholz visit Kyiv; Luhansk region under attack from ‘nine directions’
  • Giant tortoise believed extinct confirmed alive in Galápagos Islands
  • Andrée Geulen, rescuer of the Jewish children of Belgium, dies at 100
  • Kevin Spacey appears in U.K court to face sexual assault charges
  • Storm in a teacup? Pakistan urges citizens to give up drink to help economy.
  • U.S. defends weapons aid to Ukraine as ‘numbers clearly favor the Russians’
  • In Saudi crackdown on LGBTQ expression, even rainbow toys aren’t safe
  • Israel and Egypt sign gas export deal as Europe seeks Russia alternative
  • ‘Gangnam Style’ is back, but do Psy’s wild concerts have a place in covid time?
  • Leaders of France, Germany and Italy make first wartime trip to Kyiv
  • China on wrong side of history for backing Russian interests amid war, U.S. warns
  • Russian army ramps up recruitment as steep casualties thin the ranks
  • U.S. announces additional $1 billion in military aid to Ukraine
  • A dictator’s son promised unity. His election is pulling Filipinos apart.
  • Why Boris Johnson is picking fights over Irish borders, Rwandan flights
  • U.S. probing how American electronics wound up in Russian military gear
  • Man confesses to killing missing journalist and colleague, police say
  • Air pollution worse for global lifespan than cigarettes or alcohol
  • U.S. veterans missing in Ukraine, feared captured, families say
  • Turkish, Greek defense ministers meet amid renewed tensions
  • NATO to boost its forces, equipment on eastern flank
  • EXPLAINER: Why Rwanda and Congo are sliding toward war again
  • Leaders pledge arms and EU path for Ukraine in Kyiv visit
  • Hezbollah members get life terms for Lebanese leader's death
  • Abbott’s troubled baby formula factory closed again due to flooding
  • Florida fraud suspect tried to flee to Cuba on a Jet Ski, feds say
  • Charging 69 cents for gas was an ‘honest mistake’ he’s hoping to repay
  • Man illegally excavated Native American site, took artifacts, officials say
  • Children served floor sealant instead of milk at Alaska day care
  • Extreme heat and humidity kill thousands of cattle in Kansas
  • Jan. 6 hearing live updates: Pence lawyer, retired judge decry Trump’s pressure on V.P. to overturn election

  • How Americans feel about the Jan. 6 hearings so far
  • Post Politics Now: Biden asks companies to help with gas prices; Jan. 6 panel has questions about Capitol tour
  • Ginni Thomas corresponded with John Eastman, sources in Jan. 6 House investigation say
  • Mortgage rates hit 14-year high as inflation persists
  • Stock futures fall sharply, erasing post-Fed rally
  • For one job, they faced 11 interviews and 4 tasks
  • What you need to know about the tampon shortage
  • Discord adds new Twitch-like AutoMod feature to help keep users safe
  • White House to launch task force to curb online abuse
  • ‘TMNT: Shredder’s Revenge’ was made by fans for fans. Here’s how.
  • How to keep your phone working while traveling abroad
  • Rising from the ashes
  • Where do homeowners pay the most in property taxes?
  • Bank of England Joins the Scream-If-You-Wanna-Hike-Faster Gang
  • Can Revlon Be Saved With a Surge in the Lipstick Index?
  • ‘Drip Pricing’ Is Turning Checkout Into a Nasty Surprise

  • What It Would Take for Jack Ma’s Ant to Reboot an IPO
  • What’s Crypto Lending, and What Happened With Celsius?
  • What the Jan. 6 Committee Has Done, and What’s Next
  • How Europe Is Responding to Ukrainian Refugees
  • ‘Liberation is born from the soul’: Oswaldo Payá’s struggle for a free Cuba
  • QR code menus are the death of civilization
  • If Hollywood wants to stop gun violence, it should stop sanitizing it
  • QR code menus are good. No, seriously.
  • Inflation is a worldwide problem — with little immediate relief in sight
  • Juneteenth is meant to unite us, just like the Fourth of July
  • Donald Graham: Watergate resonated because The Post reported the truth
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 16 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • After 27 years, Internet Explorer is dead
  • Those spectacular sunrises and sunsets? Scientists point to Tonga volcano eruption
  • National weather forecast for Thursday June 16
  • There’s no achievable national climate target without WA action – ending Collie coal is just the start
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 16 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 16 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-16 14:18PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Incoming Hong Kong leader John Lee relies mostly on incumbent officials to fill ranks of his governing team
  • Hong Kong courts propose remote hearings for certain criminal trials, punishments for recording or broadcasting sessions
  • Veteran Hong Kong lawmaker Regina Ip set to become convenor of incoming leader John Lee’s de facto cabinet, sources say
  • Climate Change: Greater Bay Area holds the key to Hong Kong’s climate goals, delegates say at SCMP’s inaugural environmental summit
  • Suspects started clash between residents and protesters that led to elderly cleaner’s death, injured witness tells Hong Kong court
  • Company owned by Hong Kong justice chief Teresa Cheng’s husband sued for price fixing over air-conditioning works potentially worth HK$2 billion
  • Coronavirus: some patrons of Hong Kong bars adopt happy-go-lucky attitude in response to new rapid testing rule
  • Senior state officials laud ‘one country, two systems’ policy for Hong Kong, voice confidence in city’s prosperity and ‘quality democracy’
  • Going for a night out? What you need to know about Hong Kong’s new Covid rapid-test rule for bars and clubs
  • Dreaming big in a crisis: Hong Kong risk-takers didn’t let pandemic stop them starting, growing new enterprises
  • Hong Kong braces for capital outflow after 75-basis point base rate increase in lockstep with US Fed
  • Western outcry over Hong Kong’s anti-doxxing laws smacks of hypocrisy
  • Chinese scientists take ‘important step’ in stem cell therapy to treat Parkinson’s disease
  • US, China economic policies like night and day as Fed pushes interest rate skyward and PBOC eyes stabilisation
  • Taiwan admits paying American lobbyists to help establish closer US ties amid rising pressure from Beijing
  • Amazon, Cartier slap Chinese social media influencer, 8 others with lawsuits over trademark infringement, false advertising
  • China top Covid-19 fighter calls for all-in-one data portal like Europe’s EpiPulse
  • China vows private firms will receive ‘equal treatment’ in charm offensive to prop up struggling sector
  • Covid-19 in China: latest Beijing outbreak ‘easing’ after millions tested
  • Shanghai reopening: Disneyland ready for visitors once more as theme park awaits green light after Covid-19 lockdown
  • Chinese shipyard ready to launch Type 003 aircraft carrier, satellite images show
  • China debt holdings slashed by over 1 trillion yuan in May, Beijing eager to ‘safeguard’ currency
  • China-India border: state media marks second anniversary of Galwan Valley clash in lead-up to new talks
  • Taiwan shows off its latest armoured vehicle, the CM-34 Clouded Leopard, as part of home-grown defence
  • WeChat suffers glitch that stopped users from paying for lunch deliveries before noon
  • Shanghai reopening: half-open restaurants and retail stores mar city’s pledge to return to 100 per cent normality by June 30
  • China vows to crack down on online racism, spurred by new details of video featuring Malawi children
  • China’s rare ‘butterfly explosion’ at risk from ‘crazy’ tourism, ecologist warns
  • Quirky China: an animal activist performing ‘mermaid’, not any old iron man and the temple of doom
  • Leader of US House Armed Services Committee calls for both dialogue and deterrence to deal with China
  • China’s battered crypto investors appear unfazed by TerraUSD collapse, market meltdown
  • ‘No basis’ for attack fears in China’s new rules for PLA activities
  • Top US human rights official decries ‘transnational repression’ campaign by China
  • US military looks to allies to tackle ‘significant logistics challenge’ in Indo-Pacific
  • China’s party congress promotions to emphasise political security
  • Pentagon official says Beijing ‘act of aggression’ against Taiwan will draw response like Russia has seen
  • Elimination of nuclear weapons an urgent need, UN chief warns
  • How would a US recession impact Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand amid surging inflation?
  • South Korea’s nuclear expansion plans in bid to meet climate targets
  • Migrants sent US$605 billion home in 2021, more by mobiles, UN finds
  • Thailand wants selfie-taking tourists to stop abusing famous tree on island
  • Coronavirus: Thailand mulls longer hours for pubs, bars as Covid cases drop
  • Sri Lanka’s economic crisis forces many to seek passport to a better life
  • Chinese destroyer on long-distance exercises in Sea of Japan, to deter ‘attack on Taiwan’
  • North Korea expands restoration at nuclear test site to second tunnel: report
  • South Korea delays Nuri rocket launch amid glitches, heavy winds
  • India’s defiance could mean ‘dead end’ in WTO negotiations on food, vaccines, fishing
  • Coronavirus: North Korea reports another infectious disease outbreak amid Covid battle
  • In India, thousands protest ‘bulldozer justice’ against Muslims
  • Storm in a teacup as minister urges Pakistanis to drink less chai to save money
  • Ukraine war: Japanese man arrested over Vladimir Putin straw doll nailed to shrine tree
  • Kevin Spacey granted bail after ‘strenuously’ denying sex charges
  • World Cup 2022: tents in desert, no air cons, for fans as Fifa guests hog Qatar’s hotel rooms. An ‘opportunity’ to get an ‘authentic taste of Qatari camping’, official says
  • Russia blames Ukraine as Sievierodonetsk evacuation plan fails
  • Ukraine war: German, French, Italian leaders in Kyiv to show solidarity
  • UK signals it could exit Europe’s human rights convention after Rwanda deportation ruling
  • Vancouver’s housing crisis not caused by dirty money, inquiry finds
  • Saudis trolled as embassy in Washington now on ‘Jamal Khashoggi Way’
  • Elon Musk’s Boring Co is feuding with Texas over driveway regulations amid construction in rural town
  • Black Death’s ancient origins traced to lake in Central Asia
  • Anthony Fauci, Joe Biden’s top Covid-19 adviser, tests positive
  • Suspect confesses to burying bodies of UK journalist, Brazilian guide in Amazon
  • US Federal Reserve makes historic interest hike to combat inflation, may repeat in July
  • Joe Biden announces new US$1 billion in weapons for Ukraine, including Harpoon anti-ship missiles
  • Ukraine parades new French Caesar howitzer
  • Rainbow toys seized in Saudi ‘homosexuality’ crackdown
  • Blockchain gaming unicorn Animoca Brands acquires educational start-up TinyTap to build tokenised learning platform
  • New home prices in China’s tier-1 cities rise at faster pace as relief policies, easing of pandemic controls boost market
  • How China’s new complaints procedures can prevent ‘green’ ESG investments from harming local communities
  • China’s new energy firms turn to European markets for capital as US becomes increasingly hostile
  • Hong Kong stocks slide, Asian markets surrender gain as Fed’s biggest hike since 1994 reignites recession fears
  • Tech industry says ending WTO moratorium on e-commerce tariffs will hurt the economy, wants cross-border data exemption
  • Hang Seng Index: winning admirers and detractors alike as Corporate China holds sway over benchmark, returns
  • How much is China’s ‘zero-Covid’ policy cutting economic potential?
  • China’s smart EV start-up Nio seeks to challenge BMW, Audi and Mercedes-Benz with its new ES7 luxury midsize SUV
  • How China’s national computing network will be a game changer
  • Budapest makeover: how modern architecture is transforming Hungary’s capital
  • A Murder Erased movie review: Hong Kong detective mystery so abysmally conceived it has to be seen to be believed
  • What’s really happening with BTS: a ‘temporary break’ to ‘explore solo projects’ but the group ‘are not on hiatus’ and ‘will remain active’, they and their label clarify
  • What to wear to lower the risk of having cataracts – sunglasses – and what to eat: goji berries for an ‘eye-brightening effect’, according to traditional Chinese medicine. Avoid smoking, too
  • POST EDIT: New Rolex Oyster Perpetual Lady-Datejust is perfect for the modern woman – and that little bit more luxurious
  • Japanese artist returns to Hong Kong with NFTs and vivid East-meets-West paintings in Harmless Charm exhibition
  • Why most kitchen gadgets are a waste of space, used so rarely – hello, sous vide bath machine – we wonder why we bought them
  • Bling Empire’s Dorothy Wang on her health struggles, New York move and being a late bloomer
  • Top 25 Hong Kong Canto-pop singers since 1997 - interactive quiz
  • 4 rare, unique whiskies with stand-out flavour and design, from the Macallan 72 Years Old in Lalique to decades-old bottles from Glenlivet and Yamasaki
  • How did Tony Blair’s son Euan become a billionaire? Once known as a ‘bad boy’ teen, his Multiverse edtech platform is now worth US$1.6 billion – and he’s even earned an MBE from Queen Elizabeth
  • 6 K-pop idols who got cancelled for bullying scandals: from Lesserafim’s Kim Garam and Stray Kids’ Woojin, to G(I-dle)’s Soojin and AOA’s rift between Jimin and Mina
  • Why is the Cartier Tank watch so famous – and is it a good investment? Ralph Lauren, Princess Diana, Yves Saint Laurent and Catherine Deneuve all owned one for a reason
  • STYLE Edit: How luxury watchmaker Richard Mille is blazing a trail on the track with its Racing Team – from the first all-women’s team to the newest mixed driver line-up
  • Is Diana Jenkins RHOBH’s richest (and most controversial) star yet? Following fraud accusations and a raunchy book, the Real Housewives of Beverly Hills cast member’s net worth even beats Kathy Hilton
  • Will Smith and Jada Pinkett’s complicated (open) relationship: from their unconventional marriage to Jada’s ‘entanglement’ with August Alsina – but what did she say about the Oscars slap?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
